segments.text: Now when they drew near Jerusalem, to Bethphage and Bethany, at the Mount of Olives, He sent two of His disciples;
And He said to them,
Go into the village opposite you; and as soon as you have entered it you will find a colt tied, on which no one has sat. Loose it and bring it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And if anyone says to you, 'Why are you doing this?' say, 'The Lord has need of it,' and immediately he will send it here.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So they went their way, and found the colt tied by the door outside on the street, and they loosed it.
What are you doing, loosing the colt?
segments.speaker: Some standing there

segments.text: And they spoke to them just as Jesus had commanded. So they let them go.
Then they brought the colt to Jesus and threw their clothes on it, and He sat on it.
And many spread their clothes on the road, and others cut down leafy branches from the trees and spread them on the road.
Hosanna! 'Blessed is He who comes in the name of the Lord!'
segments.speaker: Those who went before and followed

segments.text: Blessed is the kingdom of our father David That comes in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest!
segments.speaker: Those who went before and followed

segments.text: And Jesus went into Jerusalem and into the temple. So when He had looked around at all things, as the hour was already late, He went out to Bethany with the twelve.
Now the next day, when they had come out from Bethany, He was hungry.
And seeing from afar a fig tree having leaves, He went to see if perhaps He would find something on it. When He came to it, He found nothing but leaves, for it was not the season for figs.
In response Jesus said to it,
Let no one eat fruit from you ever again.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And His disciples heard it.

So they came to Jerusalem. Then Jesus went into the temple and began to drive out those who bought and sold in the temple, and overturned the tables of the money changers and the seats of those who sold doves.
And He would not allow anyone to carry wares through the temple.
Then He taught, saying to them,
Is it not written, 'My house shall be called a house of prayer for all nations'? But you have made it a 'den of thieves.'
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And the scribes and chief priests heard it and sought how they might destroy Him; for they feared Him, because all the people were astonished at His teaching.
When evening had come, He went out of the city.

verses.title: The Lesson of the Withered Fig Tree

segments.text: Now in the morning, as they passed by, they saw the fig tree dried up from the roots.
And Peter, remembering, said to Him,
Rabbi, look! The fig tree which You cursed has withered away.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: So Jesus answered and said to them,
Have faith in God.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For assuredly, I say to you, whoever says to this mountain, 'Be removed and be cast into the sea,' and does not doubt in his heart, but believes that those things he says will be done, he will have whatever he says.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Therefore I say to you, whatever things you ask when you pray, believe that you receive them, and you will have them.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And whenever you stand praying, if you have anything against anyone, forgive him, that your Father in heaven may also forgive you your trespasses.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But if you do not forgive, neither will your Father in heaven forgive your trespasses.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then they came again to Jerusalem. And as He was walking in the temple, the chief priests, the scribes, and the elders came to Him.
By what authority are You doing these things? And who gave You this authority to do these things?
segments.speaker: Chief priests, scribes, and elders

segments.text: But Jesus answered and said to them,
I also will ask you one question; then answer Me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things:
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The baptism of John—was it from heaven or from men? Answer Me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And they reasoned among themselves, saying,
If we say, 'From heaven,' He will say, 'Why then did you not believe him?'
segments.speaker: Chief priests, scribes, and elders

segments.text: But if we say, 'From men'—they feared the people, for all counted John to have been a prophet indeed.
We do not know.
segments.speaker: Chief priests, scribes, and elders

segments.text: And Jesus answered and said to them,
Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things.
segments.speaker: Jesus
